title: Reproduce the large majanivirus orthogroup comparison in the web app
summary: Build a nine-genome linear comparison for large dsDNA viruses using browser LOSATP orthogroups, product-based feature colors, curved match ribbons, and a right legend.
requirements: Enough available memory for nine large viral GenBank records and LOSATP output.
The nine GenBank flat files listed below.
Use a local copy of gbdraw.app or the hosted web app; genome processing runs in the browser.

downloads.label: LC738868
downloads.note: Download the Marsupenaeus japonicus endogenous nimavirus record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: LC738874
downloads.note: Download the Melicertus latisulcatus majanivirus record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: LC738870
downloads.note: Download the Penaeus monodon majanivirus A record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: LC738873
downloads.note: Download the Penaeus semisulcatus majanivirus record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: LC738871
downloads.note: Download the Penaeus monodon majanivirus B record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: LC738872
downloads.note: Download the Litopenaeus vannamei majanivirus record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: LC738879
downloads.note: Download the Trachysalambria curvirostris majanivirus record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: LC738876
downloads.note: Download the Metapenaeus ensis majanivirus record as a GenBank flat file.

downloads.label: LC738878
downloads.note: Download the Metapenaeus joyneri majanivirus record as a GenBank flat file.

manualSteps.title: Switch to Linear mode
manualSteps.body: Open gbdraw.app and select Linear mode before adding the viral GenBank records.

media.alt: Web app header mode segmented control with Linear selected.
media.caption: Select Linear in the mode segmented control before adding the viral GenBank records.

manualSteps.title: Upload the nine GenBank files
manualSteps.body: Keep the records in the same order as the Gallery command so adjacent comparison tracks and labels match the published example.

operations.title: Linear GenBank uploader with nine viral files
operations.body: Upload the nine GenBank files in the tutorial order.

media.alt: Linear GenBank upload rows showing the nine majanivirus files in order.
media.caption: Upload the nine majanivirus GenBank files in order.

manualSteps.title: Configure browser LOSAT
manualSteps.body: In Pairwise Comparisons, choose Run LOSAT. Use LOSATP with protein blastp mode: Orthogroups.
items: Total threads: Safe for normal browser use, or 32 when mirroring the Gallery command on a capable workstation
manualSteps.note: This is a large browser workload. Start with Safe thread budgeting unless you know the browser and machine have enough memory.

operations.title: Pairwise Comparisons panel
operations.body: Open Pairwise Comparisons and choose Run LOSAT with LOSATP Orthogroups.

media.alt: Pairwise Comparisons controls with Run LOSAT, LOSATP, and Orthogroups mode selected.
media.caption: Open Pairwise Comparisons and choose Run LOSAT with LOSATP Orthogroups.

operations.title: LOSATP orthogroups mode
operations.body: Open the blastp mode selector and choose Orthogroups.

media.alt: LOSAT Settings panel with the blastp mode menu open and the Orthogroups option highlighted.
media.caption: Open blastp mode in LOSAT Settings and choose the highlighted Orthogroups option.

operations.title: Large-session thread and threshold settings
operations.body: Set Safe thread budgeting, 32 threads per run, bitscore 100, e-value 1e-3, and identity 20.

media.alt: Large-session LOSATP settings with Safe threads, 32 threads per run, bitscore 100, e-value 1e-3, and identity 20.
media.caption: Set Safe thread budgeting, 32 threads per run, bitscore 100, e-value 1e-3, and identity 20.

manualSteps.title: Set the linear layout
manualSteps.body: Use a centered, compact layout so the long viral rows and many orthogroup ribbons stay comparable.

operations.title: Linear layout controls
operations.body: Use the Layout card crop to confirm Align to Center and compact row options.

media.alt: Linear Layout card showing Align to Center and compact layout-related controls.
media.caption: Use the Layout card to confirm Align to Center and compact layout-related controls.

manualSteps.title: Set the record labels
manualSteps.body: Use descriptive record labels rather than raw accessions so the figure can be read without opening the input files.

operations.title: Record label editor
operations.body: Enter the nine descriptive record labels.

media.alt: Record label editor rows with nine descriptive majanivirus names.
media.caption: Enter the nine descriptive record labels.

manualSteps.title: Add feature color rules
manualSteps.body: Use product-based CDS rules for the broad viral protein categories visible in the Gallery legend.
items: CDS / product / wsv.*-like protein / #89d1fa / WSSV-like proteins
CDS / product / baculoviral IAP repeat-containing protein / yellow / BIRP
CDS / product / tyrosine recombinase / red / tyrosine recombinase
manualSteps.note: Use product here because the visual categories are broad protein families rather than individual genes.

operations.title: Rule 1: WSSV-like proteins
operations.body: Enter CDS / product / wsv.*-like protein / #89d1fa / WSSV-like proteins.

media.alt: SPECIFIC RULES row for CDS product wsv.*-like protein with WSSV-like proteins caption.
media.caption: Enter CDS / product / wsv.*-like protein / #89d1fa / WSSV-like proteins.

operations.title: Rule 2: BIRP
operations.body: Enter CDS / product / baculoviral IAP repeat-containing protein / yellow / BIRP.

media.alt: SPECIFIC RULES row for CDS product baculoviral IAP repeat-containing protein with BIRP caption.
media.caption: Enter CDS / product / baculoviral IAP repeat-containing protein / yellow / BIRP.

operations.title: Rule 3: tyrosine recombinase
operations.body: Enter CDS / product / tyrosine recombinase / red / tyrosine recombinase.

media.alt: SPECIFIC RULES row for CDS product tyrosine recombinase with tyrosine recombinase caption.
media.caption: Enter CDS / product / tyrosine recombinase / red / tyrosine recombinase.

operations.title: Default CDS color
operations.body: Set the default CDS color to #d3d3d3 for other proteins.

media.alt: DEFAULT COLORS grid with the CDS swatch set to #d3d3d3.
media.caption: Set the default CDS color to #d3d3d3.

manualSteps.title: Generate the diagram
manualSteps.body: Click Generate Diagram. The finished preview should show nine viral genome rows, many red identity-scaled orthogroup ribbons, and a right legend.

media.alt: Generated majanivirus orthogroup comparison in the Gallery preview.
media.caption: The large linear figure uses curved orthogroup ribbons to compare conserved protein families across nine viral genomes.

operations.title: Generated orthogroup preview
operations.body: Inspect the generated preview with nine rows + right legend.

media.alt: Actual majanivirus orthogroup tutorial UI showing Generated orthogroup preview with nine rows + right legend.
media.caption: The generated preview crop shows nine rows + right legend.

manualSteps.title: Inspect an orthogroup ribbon
manualSteps.body: Click a pairwise match ribbon to inspect the orthogroup ID, inferred display name, member count, record coverage, and member table.

media.alt: Majanivirus orthogroup comparison with an orthogroup match popup open.
media.caption: The match popup helps distinguish high-confidence shared orthogroups from dense background similarity.

operations.title: Orthogroup ribbon popup
operations.body: Click the rendered feature or match so the Orthogroup ribbon popup is visible.

media.alt: Actual majanivirus orthogroup tutorial UI showing Orthogroup ribbon popup with ribbon + popup.
media.caption: The real clicked popup crop shows Orthogroup ribbon popup with ribbon + popup.

manualSteps.title: Check the Files tab
manualSteps.body: Use the Gallery Files tab to confirm the required input filenames, accession sources, session JSON, interactive SVG, and thumbnail.

media.alt: Actual Linear Gallery tab bar with Files selected above the Files panel.
media.caption: The Gallery tab bar stays visible with Files selected above the file and artifact list.

operations.title: Files tab artifacts
operations.body: Open the Gallery Files tab; the selected tab remains visible above the file list.

media.alt: Actual Linear Gallery tab bar with Files selected above the Files panel.
media.caption: The Gallery tab bar stays visible with Files selected above the file and artifact list.

colorRules.title: Use product for broad viral protein families
colorRules.body: The visible categories in this figure are WSSV-like proteins, BIRP, tyrosine recombinase, and other proteins. Product text is the practical qualifier for those broad family rules.

colorRules.title: Keep regex values narrow enough
colorRules.body: Rules are matching patterns. wsv.*-like protein is targeted enough for this dataset, while a value such as protein would swallow most CDS features.

colorRules.title: Use default CDS color for the background
colorRules.body: Set default CDS to #d3d3d3 so the highlighted product families stand out without adding a rule for every annotation.

colorRules.title: Separate feature colors from ribbon colors
colorRules.body: Feature color rules affect genome features. Orthogroup ribbon color is controlled by pairwise match identity and the comparison style.

postGenerationEdits.title: Click match ribbons first
postGenerationEdits.body: In dense large-genome comparisons, pairwise match popups are the fastest way to confirm which orthogroup and proteins support a ribbon.

operations.title: Match ribbon popup
operations.body: Click the rendered feature or match so the Match ribbon popup is visible.

media.alt: Actual majanivirus orthogroup tutorial UI showing Match ribbon popup with ribbon + popup.
media.caption: The real clicked popup crop shows Match ribbon popup with ribbon + popup.

postGenerationEdits.title: Click CDS features before refining rules
postGenerationEdits.body: Feature popups expose product, protein_id, locus_tag, sequence, and orthogroup membership. Use those values before tightening feature color rules.

operations.title: CDS feature popup
operations.body: Click a CDS feature to inspect product, label, and color controls.

media.alt: CDS feature popup opened from the majanivirus comparison preview.
media.caption: Click a CDS feature to inspect product, label, and color controls.

postGenerationEdits.title: Keep the identity legend visible
postGenerationEdits.body: The ribbon gradient encodes match identity, so avoid deleting the pairwise match identity legend when simplifying the figure.

operations.title: Identity legend in preview
operations.body: Inspect the generated preview with identity legend.

media.alt: Actual majanivirus orthogroup tutorial UI showing Identity legend in preview with identity legend.
media.caption: The generated preview crop shows identity legend.

postGenerationEdits.title: Export SVG first
postGenerationEdits.body: Download SVG for editable vector output. PNG and PDF export can take longer because the interactive SVG contains many features and match paths.

operations.title: Export controls
operations.body: Use the Preview export toolbar to choose SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, or PDF.

media.alt: Actual Linear Preview export toolbar with the DPI selector and SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export buttons visible.
media.caption: The Preview export toolbar shows DPI plus SVG, Interactive SVG, PNG, and PDF export controls.

postGenerationEdits.title: Save a session
postGenerationEdits.body: Save a .gbdraw-session.json after edits so the web app can restore inputs, LOSATP cache, settings, generated output, and SVG adjustments.

operations.title: Session save controls
operations.body: Click Save Session in the web app header toolbar.

media.alt: Actual Linear web app header toolbar with Save Session, Load Session, Reset Settings, undo and redo, the session title, and mode controls visible.
media.caption: The app header toolbar shows Save Session in the same toolbar used to load sessions.

losatTips.title: Use the session for quick inspection
losatTips.body: This is a large example. Load the Gallery session first when the goal is to inspect the finished figure rather than benchmark a fresh LOSATP run.

losatTips.title: Start with Auto execution
losatTips.body: Auto uses threaded LOSAT only when the browser supports it and the workload justifies it.

losatTips.title: Prefer Safe total threads
losatTips.body: Safe avoids consuming every logical CPU and is the best default when you want the browser to stay responsive.

losatTips.title: Use 32 threads only deliberately
losatTips.body: The Gallery command records 32 LOSATP threads, but browser memory and worker limits vary. Use that setting only on a capable local environment.

losatTips.title: Reuse the cache
losatTips.body: Raw LOSAT results are reused when canonical inputs and LOSAT settings match, so styling-only changes should be faster after the first run.

troubleshooting.title: The browser becomes slow
troubleshooting.body: Load the Gallery session for inspection, close other heavy tabs, run LOSAT with Safe or Serial settings, or temporarily reduce the number of records while tuning layout.

troubleshooting.title: Expected ribbons are missing
troubleshooting.body: Check that protein blastp mode is Orthogroups, the input order matches the Gallery session, and the bitscore, e-value, and identity thresholds match the tutorial values.

troubleshooting.title: The rows do not match the Gallery figure
troubleshooting.body: Check Align to Center, Separate Strands, Hide length, record labels, and the nine-record input order.

troubleshooting.title: Feature categories are not colored
troubleshooting.body: Check that the product rules and default CDS color are present, and inspect a CDS popup to verify that the expected product text exists in the input annotations.

troubleshooting.title: Threaded LOSAT is unavailable
troubleshooting.body: Use Auto or Serial execution. Some browser contexts disable SharedArrayBuffer or cross-origin isolation, which prevents threaded WebAssembly.

related.label: Compare with the Hepatoplasmataceae orthogroup example
